title: Ep 6: Structure Over Headlines: Quantum Bitcoin, the Fed, and Reasoning Agents
summary: This week: the Bitcoin quantum-resistance debate and Hyperliquid challenging Wall Street, how ISM Services and the FOMC minutes set the risk backdrop, and a wave of AI research on autonomous agents that reason under uncertainty — the same discipline that separates a sharp trader from the noise.

title: Ep 5: Fused Signals Are The New Trading Moat
summary: Three signals on the shift from narratives to structure: LLM-driven strategy mutation (MadEvolve) where fused signals — not the algo — are the moat; Bitcoin miners rerating from commodity plays to AI infrastructure landlords; and $REPPO proving AI tokens can carry measurable on-chain value, not just hype.

title: Ep 4: Ai Swarms And Institutional Crypto Rotations
Ep 3: Software Development in a Per-Token Economy
summary: When every LLM call costs money, software development changes. We break down what per-token economics means for builders, traders, and the agents that run on this stuff.

title: Ep 2: From AI Demos to Hardened Systems
summary: Demos are dead, prompt engineering is dead, governance is the new moat. Three signals on what real production AI looks like in 2026.

title: Ep 1: How AI swarms broke the coding bottleneck
summary: Three stories on agentic coding: Blitzy's 10,000 parallel agents, why coding agents waste 81% of their time searching files, and OpenAI's Symphony for autonomous dispatch.
